reviewer.name: OpenAI Codex
reviewer.responsibility: Claim extraction, primary-source mapping, scope correction, and verification design

sources.title: JavaScript
sources.versionScope: Current ECMAScript and common host environments

sources.title: Document Object Model (DOM)
sources.versionScope: Current DOM standard and evergreen browsers

claims.statement: JavaScript is a programming language used by web pages and non-browser environments.
claims.applicability: Current ECMAScript; Current browser Web APIs

claims.statement: Browser page interaction uses host APIs such as the DOM in addition to the JavaScript language.
claims.sourceLocator: Language versus Web APIs; DOM introduction
claims.applicability: Current ECMAScript; Current browser Web APIs

claims.statement: Script loading and DOM selection failures can prevent intended page behavior.
claims.applicability: Current ECMAScript; Current browser Web APIs

verifications.method: Load the script and confirm it reports no syntax or module-loading error.
verifications.expectedResult: It reports no syntax or module-loading error.

verifications.method: Trace one user action through JavaScript to the resulting data or DOM change.
verifications.expectedResult: The check exposes whether javascript behaves as documented: Trace one user action through JavaScript to the resulting data or DOM change.

verifications.method: Change a known input and confirm the output changes for the expected reason.
verifications.expectedResult: The output changes for the expected reason.

limitations: The JavaScript language and browser Web APIs are related but distinct; JavaScript can run where no DOM exists.
Script loading order, modules, and document timing affect when page elements are available.
This node focuses on browser JavaScript and does not describe every JavaScript host.
Independent human review remains required before promotion to gold.
